# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Helm chart that includes an Ingress resource for our application. Configure the ingress to accept traffic from any hostname for flexibility across different environments (dev, staging, prod). We'll configure TLS certificates later when we move to production.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 # Ingress resource version (based on Kubernetes) which is compatible with the Helm package format
kind: Ingress    # Resource kind, it should match what we have defined in our helm chart definition 
metadata:        # Metadata for an object of this type. It will be embedded by kube-apiserver into resource that contains metadata such as name and namespace at creation time or on update (by updating). For Ingress objects the following fields are required, namely 'name', kind , apiVersion'. 
   name: myapp # Name can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, dots, dashes, and underscores. Cannot start with dot in Helm release's chart directory or namespace/scope (default is random). Default to the filename without extension when using defaults command of helm create --home flag
   annotations:  # Key-value pairs that can be used by users for customization as well as specific solutions related specifically. These are not interpreted directly, and they're best suited on a case basis with what you use your chart/resource (like namespace where the ingress is to reside). Annotate these values using this format: '<key>=<value>'.
     kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx  # Defaults are set in Kube-apiserver, but can be overridden if needed (nginx for example is used as ingress controller).    These values must match the annotations value otherwise it will cause error when installing helm package
     certmanager.k8s.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt  # The name of ClusterIssuer that should manage issuing certificate(default 'letsencrypt')   This is used for TLS termination, can be overwritten by users with specific settings in their charts or overlays
     nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /$1  # If the incoming request URI starts '/' followed directly from an external server then NGINX uses this to rewrite it back into a full internal path (like index, about)   Defaults are not set in nginx but can be overridden with following key/value pair for better control
metadata:      # Metadata – information that identifies the specific resource object. These details should contain enough of an identification so they will survive a reclaim and restart (default to name if defined, or otherwise defaults as unique). The namespace in which this is placed must exist on your cluster at all times for it’s existence within Kubernetes' API servers
  labels:      # Labels are key-value pairs that can be used by users who need a way around when they have found the resource (like label, selector)   These values should match what you set in Helm chart and override if necessary. For example : 'app=myweb', or even only one of them based on needs
    app: my-application  # Key is fixed ('name' field from kubernetes/metadata section), value can be anything to identify the resource object (like name, label selectors). Labels should match with helm set command for setting values in Helm chart. If not specified it won’t effect on current environment and will revert back when delete is performed
  annotations: # Annotation keys may include information like namespace where to place this ingress resource (kubernetes/metadata section) or the host that should be set while communicating with other resources of a chart. These are best used for general purposes, but might cause issues if not properly handled when using certmanager
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx  # This overrides value in Kube-apiserver's annotation (defaults to 'nginx'), and can be overwritten by users with specific settings or overlays on their charts as well if needed   These values should match the annotations section of Helm chart
    certmanager.k8s.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt  # Same functionality here, but for certificate issuance (defaults 'letsencrypt'), and can be overwritten by users with specific settings in their charts or overlays if needed   These values should match the annotations section of Helm chart
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /$1  # If incoming request URI starts '/' directly from external server then NGINX uses this to rewire it back into a full internal path (like index, about).   Defaults aren’t set in Kube and nginx but can be overridden using following key/value pairs if needed
spec:      # The specification of the Ingress resource. All sub-resources must contain fields defined here at all times – they will not merge on update unless specifically noted otherwise with 'mergeStrategy' as set below in strategic-merge-patch'. When a new field is added, it should be done via appending '-x myfieldname=myvalue', without reconfiguring the rest of Kubernetes to recognize this Ingress resource.
  rules:      # The list/array with allIngressRules that apply specific traffic patterns and either define default backend resources or have additional selectors set on them (like tls, http) for more complex use-cases    If it is not defined as an empty array at creation time then the Ingress controller won’t handle requests to any ingress resource because there's no subresource specified.
  hosts:     # List of Host entries defining host and protocol pairs where Kubernetes will send HTTP/HTTPS traffic on specific domain names (like app)   If it is not defined as an empty array at creation time then the Ingress controller won’t handle requests to any ingress resource because there's no subresource specified.
  tls:       # List of Secrets or inline reference definitions for TLS related configurations ie keys, certificates and CA bundles   If not defined as an empty array at creation time then the Ingress controller won’t handle requests to any ingress resource because there's no subresource specified.
